div1.entryFree: Daughter, female child: Tp. I, 2, 17. Tp. I, 2, 17 Tp. I, 2, 17 Gentl. II, 4, 49. Wiv. I, 1, 46 etc. etc. Eve's --s, Wiv. IV, 2, 24. that d. here of Spain, John II, 423. --s of the game, Troil. IV, 5, 63. Used for a daughter-in-law: R3 IV, 1, 5. Cor. I, 3, 1. Used by clergymen as a paternal form of addressing young women: Meas. II, 3, 30. IV, 1, 71. IV, 3, 116. Rom. IV, 1, 39. Neuter, in speaking of a new-born child: hath brought you forth a d.; here 'tis; commends it to your blessing, Wint. II, 3, 66. Rhyming to after in Shr. I, 1, 245.
